---
title: "Chap 7"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
album1<-read.delim("../Data files/Album Sales 1.dat", header = TRUE)
```

```{r}
albumSales.1 <- lm(sales ~ adverts, data = album1)
```

F-statisic with p -value of 2.2e-16. it means that the model results in significant better prediction than just mean. 
```{r}
summary(albumSales.1)
```
```{r}
album2<-read.delim("../Data files/Album Sales 2.dat", header = TRUE)
```

```{r}
albumSales.2 <- lm(sales ~ adverts, data = album2)
```

```{r}
albumSales.3 <- lm(sales ~ adverts + airplay + attract, data = album2)
summary(albumSales.3)
```
standardized beta values measured in standard deviations. make it easier to interpet. tell us the importance of each predictor. higher absolute values => more importance
```{r}
lm.beta(albumSales.3)
```
Compare 2 models to see 2nd model fits better than 1st model.
look at f-statistic and associated p-value
```{r}
anova(albumSales.2, albumSales.3)
```
```{r}

```
